title: IEEE 1584 Arc Flash Incident Energy & Boundary (dual-track: 2002 Annex F + 2018 Tables 1+3)
edition: 2018 (current) + 2002 Annex F (retained alongside for relabel correctness)
scope_one_line: Empirical arc-flash incident energy and boundary calculations for AC equipment 208 V – 15 kV. The 2018 edition (Tables 1+3 polynomial-in-V model) is the current authoritative anchor; the 2002 Annex F simplified regression is retained for traceability of pre-2018 labels.
license_note: Clause references + numeric coefficients (factual data) + brief paraphrase only — never full standard text.

edition_history.status: Annex F simplified regression retained as method-2002-annex-f-*.json for traceability + legacy label compatibility
current; Tables 1 (arc-current) + 3 (incident-energy) polynomial-in-V model is the authoritative anchor; lives in method-2018-tables-1-3-*.json

dual_track_rationale: Sprint A.3 (Path Z2-ext) on 2026-05-25 corrected a mislabel: the original method-2018-*-coefficients.json files declared 'IEEE 1584:2018' but their 7-coefficient log-linear shape (k1..k7 + x_distance_exponent) is the IEEE 1584-2002 Annex F simplified regression form, NOT the 2018 model. IEEE 1584-2018 §7 + Tables 1+3 use a 10-coef arc-current + 13-coef incident-energy polynomial-in-V form with cross-terms. The misnamed files were renamed to method-2002-annex-f-*.json (correct anchor + null values pending transcription) and the actual 2018 model files were added alongside as method-2018-tables-1-3-*.json (correct shape + null values pending transcription). Confirmed against ETAP white paper, EasyPower release notes, Bisson 'Electrical Safety: Practical Solutions' Ch. 5 — those references describe the 2018 model SHAPE (10-coef + 13-coef polynomial-in-V) but verbatim numerical coefficients require licensed access to IEEE Std 1584-2018.
